_doc: External retrieval task set (#9). Run: `python evals/run_eval.py --repo <PATH> --tasks evals/tasks.example.json [--baselines | --save <label> | --gate <label>]`. Index the target repo FIRST: `pandemonium index <PATH>`. Schema: `queries`=[{q, files (POSIX path substrings the result path must contain), symbols (bare symbol names)}]; optional `impact`=[{ref: 'path::Qualified.Name', true_direct: [refs]}] for repo_impact FP/FN. The examples below target THIS repo so the loader is runnable as-is; for the real measurement, REPLACE them with CROSS-FILE tasks authored for your LARGE repo — the regime where the protocol is predicted to win. NOTE: this measures RETRIEVAL RANKING (precision@k/MRR). The token/cost-at-scale win is AGENT-level and lives in evals/ab_runner.py (seeded-bug tasks) — see docs RESULTS.md '#9' for the remaining half.

queries.q: trace how a search query flows from the CLI through the retriever into the stores
how is a low-confidence result set detected and then recovered by fanning out
where does indexing turn a parsed symbol into an embedded descriptor
